what_it_does: Implements the ViIdentity overlay UI: a React component suite that projects the MS_IDENTITY registry and related single-source registries as an interactive identity dashboard. It renders Vi's live composed prompt, memory recall, decision dials, makeable kinds, generated artifact cards, streams, accumulated leans, behaviours, registry network, and a persistent floating Vi chat console, wiring edits back through global registry APIs.
observations: The top comment frames the file as a projection layer that renders MS_IDENTITY rather than re-declaring it.
IdentityProjection is a switch that maps aspect.projection values to dedicated ID* components.
IDConsole reads from window.useViChat and applies Vi's proposed action verbs (stream, generate, behaviour, facet) via chat.apply.
IDOverview is also exposed as window.ViOverview for reuse on other surfaces.
IDComposed displays the literal system prompt sections that MS_VI_BRAIN composes for the current context.
FacetCard lets users rewrite the disposition text and add/remove leans for an identity zone.
IDRecall supports adding memories, adjusting weight, and forgetting them through MS_MEMORY.
IDDials reuses window.DSSpecimen for option previews and commits choices via setTweak.
ArtifactContent generically renders produced content fields and detects hex-color arrays for swatches.
window.CandidateCard is defined as the single reusable rendering of a generated part.
IDKinds generates artifacts through MS_PRODUCE.create and shows them with CandidateCard.
IDStreams delegates to the shared StreamRunner component when a sequence is running.
IDLeans surfaces variant-axis biases and the prefer/avoid signals behind them.
IDArtifacts groups artifacts by kind and lets users advance status through a simple cycle.
Many components read stores from window globals and call registry mutation methods directly.
The file is truncated near the end, so the full wrapper component and tail functions are not visible.
standouts: Persistent Vi chat column that can mutate identity registries through action verbs.
CandidateCard as the one reusable artifact card across surfaces.
Live composed-prompt viewer that exposes the exact prompt Vi is using.
Overview dashboard that surfaces pending candidate decisions as the primary action.
Registry network visualization with circular node layout.
conventions: Uses window.* singletons for stores and APIs instead of ES module imports.
Functional React components with useState/useEffect aliased as useStateID/useEffectID.
Component names prefixed with ID for identity-aspect views.
Projection engine pattern: switch on aspect.projection to dispatch to aspect components.
Reuses existing studio components (MemoryAspect, DSSpecimen, KindSpecimen, StreamRunner, ViThread).
Exposes reusable components on window (IdentityProjection, ViOverview, CandidateCard, ArtifactContent).
Direct registry mutation in event handlers via setTweak/setFacet/addLearning/etc.
concerns: Heavy reliance on window globals makes static typing, testing, and dependency tracing difficult.
Large file mixes many responsibilities: chat, dashboard, memory, generation, streams, behaviours, network.
Some handler functions mutate global registries directly without visible error handling.
A few null checks are implicit (e.g., window.MS_DECISIONS.activeOption, window.MS_RESOLVE.axisFor).
Duplicate simple names like add/save are disambiguated only by line suffixes, suggesting local naming collisions.
File is truncated, so the full lifecycle of the main wrapper and tail functions is unknown.
notes: This is the live identity dashboard surface: a projection layer over the MS_IDENTITY registry that composes existing studio components rather than rebuilding them.
questions: Where are MS_IDENTITY, MS_VI_BRAIN, MS_PRODUCE, MS_STREAMS, and the other window globals defined?
What is the exact shape of ctx and which fields are guaranteed when passed into these aspects?
How does chat.apply implement the action verbs (generate, behaviour, facet, stream) and what does it return?
What stylesheet defines the idy-* class names?
What is the full behaviour of the truncated tail (IDNetwork, main wrapper, ftarget)?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — render MS_IDENTITY as a live mirror, compose existing surfaces, and write edits back to the registries — matches the implementation: every aspect reads from global registry APIs and writes through them, and existing components are reused where possible.
apparent_themes: Closing the learning loop: teach → generate → decide → lean
Transparency: show the prompt, provenance, and leans
intention_signals: Turn Vi's identity from a static settings panel into a live, inspectable, editable control plane; make the AI's current context and composed prompt visible and actionable; unify generation, memory, decisions, and chat in one overlay; let users teach and refine the assistant in place.
novelty: A unified identity overlay that exposes the literal system prompt being composed, visualizes the registry network, and surfaces candidate decisions as the primary action, with a persistent chat that can mutate identity registries via action verbs.
contribution: A reusable projection engine and set of aspect views that render identity, memory, decisions, artifacts, and generation surfaces from shared registries; reusable components such as CandidateCard, ArtifactContent, and ViOverview; and a pattern for conversational registry editing through action verbs.
summary_for_embedding: identity-ui.jsx implements the ViIdentity overlay as a React projection over MS_IDENTITY and related registries, providing interactive aspects for overview, live composed prompt, memory recall, decision dials, makeable kinds, artifact cards, streams, leans, behaviours, and a persistent Vi chat console.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single, live, editable mirror of Vi's identity and context where changes immediately feed back into the shared registry system.

symbols.does: Persistent Vi chat column rendered alongside every identity aspect.
symbols.description: Reads messages and busy state from window.useViChat, composes a greeting from MS_VI_BRAIN.where, sends user messages with withActions:true, and applies Vi's returned action verbs (stream, generate, behaviour, facet) via chat.apply while jumping to the relevant aspect so the effect is visible.

symbols.does: Builds the console's initial greeting string.
symbols.description: Uses brain.where(ctx) to name the current project or scope and invites the user to ask questions or request changes to the currently viewed aspect.

symbols.does: Sends the current input through the Vi chat hook.
symbols.description: Trims the input (or an optional text argument), calls chat.send with withActions:true, and clears the local input field.

symbols.does: Applies a proposed action from Vi's reply.
symbols.description: Routes stream actions through onAct, otherwise calls chat.apply with setTweak and jumps to artifacts, disposition, or behaviours so the user sees the result.

symbols.does: UI for teaching Vi a standing learning.
symbols.description: Lists learning kinds such as prefer/avoid/should_ask, provides a scope selector and text area, commits a new learning to MS_FEEDBACK, and shows recent learnings with remove buttons.

symbols.does: Commits a new learning to MS_FEEDBACK.
symbols.description: Validates the teach text and calls MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning with the selected scope, kind, and text, then clears the form and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Live actionable dashboard aspect.
symbols.description: Pulls counts and lists from MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_FEEDBACK, MS_MEMORY, MS_IDENTITY, and MS_VI_BRAIN; surfaces pending candidate decisions with CandidateCard; summarizes leans; lists recent activity; and provides quick-action buttons that jump to other aspects. Also exported as window.ViOverview.

symbols.does: Computes a lean summary inside IDOverview.
symbols.description: Maps over window.MS_VARY, finds kinds whose labels match prefer signals, resolves the current variant bias, and returns the leaned axis value for display in the overview.

symbols.does: Renders the live composed system prompt.
symbols.description: Displays prompt sections returned by read(ctx), including section count, approximate word count, model label, and each section's source aspect with a jump-to-editor button.

symbols.does: Renders editable identity facet cards per zone.
symbols.description: Calls read(ctx) to obtain facets and marks the card matching the active context zone; delegates rendering and editing to FacetCard.

symbols.does: Editable card for one identity facet.
symbols.description: Shows the zone icon, label, active indicator, disposition text (click to edit), save/cancel/reset controls, and a list of leans that can be added or removed via MS_IDENTITY.setFacet.

symbols.does: Saves an edited facet disposition.
symbols.description: Persists the edited disposition text to MS_IDENTITY.setFacet for the facet id, closes editing mode, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Adds a lean to a facet.
symbols.description: Appends a new lean string to the facet's leans array via MS_IDENTITY.setFacet and clears the input.

symbols.does: Removes a lean from a facet.
symbols.description: Removes the lean at index i from the facet's leans array via MS_IDENTITY.setFacet.

symbols.does: Renders context awareness rows.
symbols.description: Displays rows returned by read(ctx) as an awareness list, or an empty state when no rows exist.

symbols.does: Memory recall and curation aspect.
symbols.description: Groups remembered items by topic, lets the user add a new memory with topic and content, adjust weight up/down, or forget an item via MS_MEMORY.

symbols.does: Adds a new memory inside IDRecall.
symbols.description: Validates content and calls MS_MEMORY.learn with origin 'stated', the given topic, content, and weight 1.2, then clears inputs and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Renders decision dials as interactive option grids.
symbols.description: For each decision id, fetches the decision from MS_DECISIONS, determines the active option, renders options using DSSpecimen for previews, and commits a choice via setTweak with a toast.

symbols.does: Generic renderer for an artifact's produced content.
symbols.description: Handles pending, generating, and error content states; toggles between current and previous content; renders headline, body, and list fields; and detects hex-color arrays to render swatches. Exported as window.ArtifactContent.

symbols.does: Predicate for hex-color strings.
symbols.description: Returns true when a value is a string matching a hex color pattern with an optional leading #.

symbols.does: Extracts a display label from a content list item.
symbols.description: Falls back through common fields such as label, title, name, headline, screen, step, or the first string value.

symbols.does: Extracts a secondary line from a content list item.
symbols.description: Prefers a numeric value, then role, body, action, sub, or hex fields.

symbols.does: Compact inline refinement input.
symbols.description: Expands to a text input for a refinement steer and invokes onRefine when Enter is pressed; closes on Escape.

symbols.does: Makeable-kinds generation aspect.
symbols.description: Lists resolvable kind specimens, generates a new candidate artifact via MS_PRODUCE.create (with MS_ARTIFACTS.create fallback), and displays the resulting CandidateCards with provenance trace and refine controls.

symbols.does: Creates a candidate artifact for a kind.
symbols.description: Uses MS_VI_BRAIN.genContext to build generation context, creates the artifact, adds it to local 'made' state, and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Streams listing and runner aspect.
symbols.description: Lists available sequences with run buttons; when one is selected, renders the shared StreamRunner component with site context and setTweak, otherwise shows the list.

symbols.does: Surfaces accumulated variant preferences.
symbols.description: For each kind with a variant axis, computes the current variant bias and the prefer/avoid signals that mention the kind, and lets the user clear those learnings.

symbols.does: Clears learnings for a kind inside IDLeans.
symbols.description: Removes learnings whose text mentions the kind label and whose kind is prefer/avoid, then shows a toast.

symbols.does: Artifacts-by-kind gallery aspect.
symbols.description: Groups all artifacts by kind, shows a stream-made banner if applicable, offers an Open in Composition button, and lets users advance each artifact's status through a simple cycle.

symbols.does: Renders teach/feedback/iterate actions.
symbols.description: Filters and deduplicates actions returned by read() to those in the learn, feedback, or iterate groups.

symbols.does: Behaviour CRUD aspect.
symbols.description: Shows the active behaviour count, toggles an add form, and creates a new standing rule via store.addBehaviour.

symbols.does: Adds a new behaviour inside IDBehaviours.
symbols.description: Validates the new behaviour text and label and calls store.addBehaviour, then clears the form and shows a toast.

symbols.does: Renders a single behaviour row.
symbols.description: Displays a behaviour with edit/toggle/remove controls; uses the identity store to update or delete the behaviour. The surrounding file is truncated.

symbols.does: Saves an edited behaviour inside BehaviourRow.
symbols.description: Calls store.setBehaviourText with the trimmed text and closes editing mode.

symbols.does: Renders the identity registry network.
symbols.description: Visualizes registry nodes returned by read as a circular graph, with optional big layout and onJump navigation when nodes are activated.

symbols.does: Computes a node's circular SVG position.
symbols.description: Calculates x/y coordinates on a circle given the node index and total node count.

symbols.does: Returns a status/count string for a registry node.
symbols.description: Wraps the count lookup in a try/catch and returns a readable status string.

symbols.does: Navigates from a network node.
symbols.description: Calls onJump with the node's aspect id when a network node is activated.

symbols.does: Handles stream completion event.
symbols.description: Listens to a custom event detail containing produced ids and likely updates local stream-made state when a stream finishes.

symbols.does: Handles action objects near the file end.
symbols.description: If the action type is 'stream', sets the running stream id and selected aspect to 'streams'.

symbols.does: Switches the selected aspect.
symbols.description: Updates the selected aspect id and clears any running stream unless the target itself is 'streams'.

symbols.does: Maps an aspect to a feedback target near the truncated end.
symbols.description: Appears to construct a feedback target object using fb.target with global scope and specimen type; the full logic is cut off in the truncated file.
